Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase OleY/MycE family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-olivosyl-oleandolide + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = L-oleandrosyl-oleandolide + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:31623, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:29613, ChEBI:CHEBI:29614, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.239; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:11514520};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: 3-O-methyltransferase involved in the synthesis of L-oleandrose, a sugar attached to oleandomycin, a macrolide antibiotic. Acts on monoglycosylated macrolactones and mediates the conversion of L-olivosyl-erythronolide B into its 3-O-methylated derivative, L-oleandrosyl-erythronolide B. Also able to methylate other monoglycosylated derivatives, such as L-rhamnosyl- and L-mycarosyl-erythronolide B. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:11514520}.
